location.office_time_required: must live within commuting distance of a hub; in-person time valued for planning, problem-solving, and connection

level_signals: 3+ years of experience in strategy and operations roles
Reports into VP of Commerce function

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field
3+ years of experience in strategy and operations roles (e.g., consulting, finance, GM, Operations)
Excellent project management skills, with the ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously
Proficient in data analysis and reporting tools
Ability to think strategically and execute methodically across multiple workstreams
Proficient with SQL and analytics tools like Looker, Sigma, Hex

scope.surface: Commerce post-purchase operations — logistics, payments, user experience, trust and fraud
scope.business_model: marketplace / livestream shopping platform, B2C and B2B (seller-side)
core_responsibilities: Develop and implement commerce strategies that drive margin optimization and/or growth across the post-purchase experience
Manage cross-functional commerce projects in close partnership with the product, engineering, and policy teams
Identify inefficiencies in processes and implement improvements to enhance productivity/reduce costs
Analyze commerce data to provide insights and recommendations
Negotiate contracts and manage vendor performance to ensure quality and cost-effectiveness

green_flags: Forbes #1 Best Startup Employer in America — strong employer brand signal
Role has clear scope reporting into VP of Commerce with well-defined ownership areas
Multiple hub city options (SF, LA, Phoenix, NYC) offer geographic flexibility
Comprehensive benefits including parental leave, family planning, wellness, and dogfooding budget
Explicit mention of 401k employer match (up to 4% of base salary)
red_flags: Must live within commuting distance of a hub city — not fully remote despite 'remote co-located' framing
Visa sponsorship and work authorization policy not stated
No mention of equity, which is unusual for a high-growth startup role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Associate' and 3+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as livestream shopping marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned set to false (not mentioned anywhere in JD body)
ambiguities: JD describes Whatnot as a 'remote co-located team' but also requires hub proximity for commuting — treated as hybrid here
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD; marked 'unclear'
